words.meaning_en: Giving a lot of information in few words
words.example_sentence: The teacher asked for a concise summary of the chapter.
words.fill_blank: A ______ answer is better than a long, confusing one.

words.meaning_en: To say the opposite of what someone else has said
words.example_sentence: The new evidence contradicted the earlier findings.
words.fill_blank: His actions ______ his words every single time.

words.meaning_en: To represent something as larger or better than it really is
words.example_sentence: He tends to exaggerate when he tells stories.
words.fill_blank: Don't ______ your achievements — honesty is better.

words.meaning_en: So delicate or precise as to be difficult to see; not obvious
words.example_sentence: There was a subtle difference between the two paintings.
words.fill_blank: His ______ humor was not understood by everyone.

words.meaning_en: Producing powerful images in the mind; bright and strong
words.example_sentence: She had a vivid memory of her first day at school.
words.fill_blank: The poet used ______ imagery to describe the storm.

words.example_sentence: She was articulate and confident during the interview.
words.fill_blank: An ______ speaker can inspire large audiences.

words.example_sentence: Her essay was well-structured and coherent.
words.fill_blank: Present your ideas in a ______ manner during the debate.

words.meaning_en: A formal discussion on a topic where opposing views are expressed
words.example_sentence: The school held a debate on the use of social media.
words.fill_blank: There was a heated ______ over the new school policy.

words.meaning_en: The practice of taking someone else's work and passing it off as one's own
words.example_sentence: Copying from the internet without reference is plagiarism.
words.fill_blank: Students must avoid ______ in their projects.

words.meaning_en: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter in hand
words.example_sentence: Give only relevant information in your answer.
words.fill_blank: Include only ______ points in your essay.

words.meaning_en: A figure of speech in which one thing is described as being something else to suggest a resemblance
words.example_sentence: The phrase 'life is a journey' is a metaphor comparing life to travel.
words.fill_blank: The poet used a ______ to describe time as a thief.

words.meaning_en: A story in which characters and events represent abstract ideas or moral lessons
words.example_sentence: George Orwell's Animal Farm is an allegory of the Russian Revolution.
words.fill_blank: Plato's cave is a famous ______ about the nature of knowledge and reality.

words.meaning_en: A group of lines forming a unit in a poem, similar to a paragraph in prose
words.example_sentence: The poem had five stanzas, each exploring a different emotion.
words.fill_blank: Each ______ of the poem ended with a rhyming couplet.

words.meaning_en: A spoken or written account of a series of connected events; a story
words.example_sentence: The narrative of the novel was told from the child's perspective.
words.fill_blank: The first-person ______ makes the story feel personal and immediate.

words.meaning_en: The use of words to convey the opposite of their literal meaning, or a situation opposite to what is expected
words.example_sentence: It was an irony that the fire station caught fire.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the story was that the detective was himself the criminal.

words.meaning_en: The main character in a story, novel, film, or play
words.example_sentence: The protagonist of the novel faces great hardship before achieving his goal.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the story overcomes many hardships to reach her goal.

words.meaning_en: The use of symbols to represent ideas, qualities, or concepts beyond their literal meaning
words.example_sentence: In the poem, the dove is used as symbolism for peace.
words.fill_blank: The green light in The Great Gatsby is considered ______ for the American Dream.
